channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-19
item.description:
Our community has seen exciting progress across multiple projects recently. In the **Davidvandijcke/coarse** project, significant updates were made to the 'dev' branch, including localization of the confirmation email and review page, as well as a crucial fix for deployment. Meanwhile, the **JamieCummins/regcheck** project focused on enhancing the staging branch, with improvements to the qwen feature, logging functionality, and efforts to resolve an osf bug. Notably, the updates in **regcheck** were pushed for redeployment, demonstrating the project's momentum. These advancements highlight the connections between our projects, as both **coarse** and **regcheck** prioritize user experience and reliability. As our community continues to drive innovation, we celebrate the tireless efforts of our contributors and look forward to the impact of these updates.

123 commits across 7 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Dormant repos revived: output-it-forward

Most active:

regcheck37 commits
coarse31 commits
docpluck29 commits
metacheck23 commits
escicheck1 commits

item.title: metacheck: 2 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The `fix/code-check-paperlist` branch was updated to address a specific issue, where the code_check was crashing and the paper_id was missing on paper lists, as stated in the commit message "Fix code_check crash and missing paper_id on paper lists". The `papers_databases` branch saw the addition of new functions, specifically to "load paper databases from scienceverse/papers", according to the commit message. Both of these changes were made by Lakens, as indicated by the commit messages. The work was focused on two distinct areas: fixing a crash issue and adding database loading functionality. These changes were made on the same day, June 19, 2026.

2 commit(s) today:

  • 8cbef44 [fix/code-check-paperlist] Fix code_check crash and missing paper_id on paper lists — Lakens
  • b7afa6a [papers_databases] add functions to load paper databases from scienceverse/papers — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 2 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `main` branch was worked on, where documentation was updated to note that the pre-push canary hook should "never timeout/kill the push". On the `main` branch, a feature was added for tables, referred to as REQUEST_10, which includes "HTTP-flatten enabling quality fixes + cross-flavor capture" and is associated with version 2.4.94. Only the `main` branch was modified in these commits. The changes made to the `main` branch include both documentation and feature additions. No other branches were worked on in these commits.

2 commit(s) today:

  • 83ab6bf [main] docs(deploy): document pre-push canary hook timing — never timeout/kill the push — Gilad Feldman
  • 1158b1b [main] feat(tables): REQUEST_10 — HTTP-flatten enabling quality fixes + cross-flavor capture (v2.4.94) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: coarse: 5 commits today
item.description:
Davidvandijcke/coarse

Work was done on the 'main' and 'dev' branches. On the 'dev' branch, the confirmation email was localized and the review page now follows the review language, as stated in the commit message "fix(i18n): localize confirmation email + review page follows review language (#244)". The 'dev' branch also had a fix for deployment, specifically enabling RLS on the schema_migrations bookkeeping table, as per the commit "fix(deploy): enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table (#243)". A merge was performed on the 'main' branch, incorporating changes from the 'dev' branch via pull request #245. Additionally, the 'dev' branch was updated by merging remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into sync/reconcile-v1.8.0, and it also merged pull request #242 from the 'release/v1.8.0' branch.

5 commit(s) today:

  • 05aad76 [main] Merge pull request #245 from Davidvandijcke/dev — David Van Dijcke
  • 49a72b3 [dev] Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into sync/reconcile-v1.8.0 — Davidvandijcke
  • 2e86c5f [dev] fix(i18n): localize confirmation email + review page follows review language (#244) — David Van Dijcke
  • 907c629 [dev] Merge pull request #242 from Davidvandijcke/release/v1.8.0 — David Van Dijcke
  • f54b584 [dev] fix(deploy): enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table (#243) — David Van Dijcke

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 6 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The staging branch was the focus of the recent work, with multiple commits made to it. The commit "improve qwen" suggests that changes were made to the qwen feature, although the specifics of the improvement are not detailed. Another commit, "re-add qwen!", indicates that the qwen feature was reinstated after potentially being removed previously. A commit titled "hunting down osf bug" implies that efforts were made to identify and possibly fix an issue related to the osf, but the outcome is not specified. Additionally, the commit "improve logging" suggests that modifications were made to the logging functionality to enhance its effectiveness. The "push for redeploy" commit implies that the changes made were pushed for redeployment, likely to apply the updates to a live environment.

6 commit(s) today:

  • f775856 [staging] improve qwen — Jamie Cummins
  • 724d020 [staging] re-add qwen! — Jamie Cummins
  • 89a1470 [staging] push for redeploy — Jamie Cummins
  • baebc81 [staging] hunting down osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 2f5eaaa [staging] improve logging — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: output-it-forward: activity update
item.description:
giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward

The `main` branch was updated with a new commit that added a Chrome Webstore link for OIF, as stated in the commit message "Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF". No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. The commit specifically added a link, with no other changes mentioned. There were no fixes or new modules added in this set of commits, only the addition of the Chrome Webstore link. The work was limited to a single commit on the `main` branch. No other features were changed or added in this update.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
